focus_verification.F1_monotonic_guard_challenges_currentPro: RESOLVED — src/stores/useChallengeStore.ts hydrateFromRemote loops per-row, compares local.currentProgress > remoteRow.currentProgress, preserves local when ahead.
focus_verification.F2_stale_supabase_types_imports: RESOLVED — only doc-comment references in tableRegistry.ts; grep finds zero active imports of supabase/types.
focus_verification.F3_hydrateP3P4_awaited_before_syncEngine: RESOLVED — useAuthLifecycle.ts awaits hydrateP3P4FromSupabase (with .catch) before syncEngine.start per ordering comment.
focus_verification.F4_deletePath_rollback_pattern: RESOLVED — useLearningPathStore.deletePath captures prevPaths/prevEntries/prevActivePath snapshot pre-mutation; catch restores full snapshot.
focus_verification.F5_dead_var_markAllRead: STILL PRESENT — unreadIds computed at line 159 used only for early-return; unread recomputed at line 166 (minor duplication).
focus_verification.F6_quiz_hydration_gap: STILL PRESENT (intentional) — hydrateP3P4.ts explicitly skips quizzes; no useQuizStore.hydrateFromRemote exists. Documented in code comment.

findings.title: quizzes LWW table has no hydrate path on sign-in
findings.why: Per plan, quizzes were wired for upload via syncableWrite (batch C) but the download/hydrate side is deferred. New device sign-in will not pull existing quizzes until a future story adds useQuizStore.hydrateFromRemote. This is a documented, intentional gap but remains a user-visible sync incompleteness.
findings.suggestedFix: Create follow-up story to add useQuizStore.hydrateFromRemote (bulkPut-based, no syncableWrite), then dispatch from hydrateP3P4FromSupabase. Update plan §Scope Boundaries or file known-issue.

findings.title: Duplicate unread filter in markAllRead
findings.why: unreadIds is computed at line 159 solely for the length-check guard; the loop body re-filters notifications at line 166 to build `unread`. Minor redundancy — one filter pass suffices.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace line 166 `const unread = notifications.filter(n => n.readAt === null)` by filtering once and reusing, or compute `const unread = notifications.filter(n => n.readAt === null); if (unread.length === 0) return; const unreadIds = unread.map(n => n.id)` to eliminate the duplicate scan.
